Question 860 [Math] - ID: sat-math-2e8027b0
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: Two-variable data: Models and scatterplots
------------------------------------------------------------
The scatterplot shows the relationship between two variables, x and y, for data set E. A line of best fit passes through (0, 12) and (12, 30). Data set F is created by multiplying the y-coordinate of each data point from data set E by 3.9. Which of the following could be an equation of a line of best fit for data set F?

  A. y = 46.8 + 5.9x ✓ CORRECT
  B. y = 12 + 1.5x
  C. y = 30 + 3.9x
  D. y = 12 + 1.5x

Explanation: The line for data set E passes through (0, 12) and (12, 30). For data set F, y-coordinates are multiplied by 3.9. The new y-intercept is 12 × 3.9 = 46.8. The new point at x = 12 is (12, 30 × 3.9) = (12, 117). The slope is (117 - 46.8)/(12 - 0) = 70.2/12 = 5.85 ≈ 5.9. So the equation is y = 46.8 + 5.9x.

Question 861 [Math] - ID: sat-math-c178d4da
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
------------------------------------------------------------
Data set A and data set B each consist of 10 values. Data set A has more values clustered near the mean than data set B. Which of the following statements best compares the means and standard deviations of the two data sets?

  A. The mean of A equals the mean of B, and the standard deviation of A is less than that of B. ✓ CORRECT
  B. The mean of A equals the mean of B, and the standard deviation of A is greater than that of B.
  C. The mean of A is greater than the mean of B, and the standard deviations are equal.
  D. The mean of A is less than the mean of B, and the standard deviations are equal.

Explanation: Standard deviation measures the spread of data. If data set A has more values clustered near the mean compared to data set B, then data set A has less spread and therefore a smaller standard deviation.

Question 862 [Math] - ID: sat-math-d6af3572
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: Two-variable data: Models and scatterplots
------------------------------------------------------------
The scatterplot shows the federal-mandated minimum wage every 10 years between 1940 and 2010. A line of best fit is shown with equation y = 0.096x - 0.49. What does the line of best fit predict about the increase in the minimum wage over the 70-year period?

  A. Each year between 1940 and 2010, the average increase in minimum wage was 0.096 dollars. ✓ CORRECT
  B. Each year between 1940 and 2010, the average increase in minimum wage was 0.49 dollars.
  C. Every 10 years between 1940 and 2010, the average increase in minimum wage was 0.096 dollars.
  D. Every 10 years between 1940 and 2010, the average increase in minimum wage was 0.488 dollars.

Explanation: The slope of the line of best fit is 0.096. From the definition of slope, an increase of 1 in x corresponds to an increase of 0.096 in y. Since x represents years, the minimum wage increases by 0.096 dollars per year.

Question 863 [Math] - ID: sat-math-c0ef9aa1

------------------------------------------------------------
A sample of oak has a density of 807 kilograms per cubic meter. The sample is in the shape of a cube, where each edge has a length of 0.9 meters. To the nearest whole number, what is the mass, in kilograms, of this sample?

  A. 588 ✓ CORRECT
  B. 726
  C. 807
  D. 897

Explanation: The volume of the cube is 0.9³ = 0.729 cubic meters. Mass = density × volume = 807 × 0.729 = 588.303 kilograms, which rounds to 588 kilograms.

  A. 2
  B. 3
  C. 4 ✓ CORRECT
  D. 5

Explanation: The range of Class A is 5 - 0 = 5. The range of Class B is 10 - 1 = 9. The positive difference between the ranges is 9 - 5 = 4.

Which of the following statements must be true? I. The median of data set A is equal to the median of data set B. II. The standard deviation of data set A is equal to the standard deviation of data set B.

  A. I only ✓ CORRECT
  B. II only
  C. I and II
  D. Neither I nor II

Question 866 [Math] - ID: sat-math-e7133228
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
------------------------------------------------------------
The speed of a vehicle is increasing at a rate of 7.3 meters per second squared. Using 1 mile = 1,609 meters, what is this rate, in miles per minute squared, rounded to the nearest tenth?

  A. 0.3
  B. 16.3 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 29.4
  D. 438.8

Explanation: Convert 7.3 m/s² to miles/min². There are 60 seconds in 1 minute, so 60² = 3,600 s² in 1 min². Rate = (7.3 meters/s²) × (1 mile/1609 meters) × (3600 s²/1 min²) = 7.3 × 3600/1609 ≈ 16.33 miles/min².

Question 867 [Math] - ID: sat-math-241f1db7
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
------------------------------------------------------------
A frequency table summarizes weights of 71 tortoises. A weight of 39 pounds is added, creating a new data set of 72 tortoises. The median of the original data set is 17 (the 36th value). Which statement best compares the mean and median of the new data set to the original?

  A. The mean of the new data set is greater than the original, and the median is greater.
  B. The mean of the new data set is greater than the original, and the medians are equal. ✓ CORRECT
  C. The mean of the new data set is less than the original, and the median is less.
  D. The mean of the new data set is less than the original, and the medians are equal.

Explanation: Adding 39 (greater than any existing value) increases the mean. For the new data set with 72 values, the median is between the 36th and 37th values. Since 39 is added at the end, both the 36th and 37th values remain 17, so the median stays 17.

Question 868 [Math] - ID: sat-math-1fbd3b67
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: Percentages
------------------------------------------------------------
The number b is 80% less than 24. The number a is 190% greater than b. What is the value of a?

  A. 9.12
  B. 13.92 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 26.40
  D. 45.60

Explanation: If b is 80% less than 24, then b = 24 - 0.80(24) = 24 - 19.2 = 4.8. If a is 190% greater than b, then a = b + 1.90b = 2.90b = 2.90(4.8) = 13.92.

------------------------------------------------------------

  A. 17
  B. 22
  C. 28
  D. 32 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: With 30 restaurants, the median is between the 15th and 16th values. Cumulative frequencies: 0-9 (2), 10-19 (7), 20-29 (15), 30-39 (25). The 15th value is in the 20-29 range and the 16th is in the 30-39 range, so the median could be in the 30-39 range. 32 is the only choice in that range.

  A. 12 ✓ CORRECT
  B. 15
  C. 240
  D. 340

Explanation: Increasing x by 400% means x + 4x = 5x = 60. Dividing by 5: x = 12.

  B. 0.64

According to this estimate, how many additional people per day are predicted to visit the beach for each 5°C increase in average temperature?

  A. 57
  B. 114

  D. 570

Question 873 [Math] - ID: sat-math-947a3cde
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Lines, angles, and triangles
------------------------------------------------------------
In the figure, lines M and R intersect at point P. Angle MPR is supplementary to an angle of 60°, and another angle in triangle MPR is 70°. If the triangle is isosceles with two equal angles, what is the measure, in degrees, of the smallest angle?

  A. 25
  B. 30 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 35
  D. 40

Explanation: If angle MPR's supplement is 60°, then angle MPR = 120°. The exterior angle of 60° equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles. If the triangle is isosceles with these two angles equal, each is 60°/2 = 30°.

Question 874 [Math] - ID: sat-math-a4c0547f
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Right triangles and trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
In triangle XYZ, angle Z is a right angle and the length of YZ is 24 units. If tan(X) = 12/35, what is the perimeter, in units, of triangle XYZ?

  A. 84
  B. 168 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 112
  D. 196

Explanation: tan(X) = opposite/adjacent = YZ/XZ = 24/XZ = 12/35. Cross-multiply: 24 × 35 = 12 × XZ, so XZ = 840/12 = 70. Using Pythagorean theorem: XY² = 70² + 24² = 4900 + 576 = 5476, so XY = 74. Perimeter = 24 + 70 + 74 = 168.


================================================================================

Question 875 [Math] - ID: sat-math-983412ea
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Area and volume
------------------------------------------------------------
A right square prism has a height of 14 units. The volume of the prism is 2,016 cubic units. What is the length, in units, of an edge of the base?

  A. 10
  B. 12 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 14
  D. 16

Explanation: Volume = s²h, where s is the edge length of the square base. 2016 = s² × 14. Solving: s² = 2016/14 = 144, so s = 12 units.

Question 876 [Math] - ID: sat-math-e5c57163
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Area and volume
------------------------------------------------------------
Square A has side lengths that are 166 times the side lengths of square B. The area of square A is k times the area of square B. What is the value of k?

  A. 166
  B. 332
  C. 13,778
  D. 27,556 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: If the side of square B is x, then the side of square A is 166x. Area of B = x², Area of A = (166x)² = 27,556x². So A's area is 27,556 times B's area, meaning k = 27,556.

Question 877 [Math] - ID: sat-math-f88f27e5
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Lines, angles, and triangles
------------------------------------------------------------
Intersecting lines r, s, and t form a triangle. One angle is 106°, another is 23°. An exterior angle x is formed. If the exterior angle equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles (one being 23° and the other being supplementary to 106°), what is the value of x?

  A. 83
  B. 97 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 106
  D. 129

Explanation: The angle supplementary to 106° is 180° - 106° = 74°. The exterior angle x equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles: x = 23° + 74° = 97°.

  A. 176 ✓ CORRECT
  B. 488
  C. 588
  D. 1,355

------------------------------------------------------------
A scatterplot shows the numbers of grams of both total protein and total fat for eight sandwiches on a restaurant menu. The line of best fit passes through approximately (20, 30) and (27, 40.5). According to the line of best fit, which is closest to the predicted increase in total fat, in grams, for every increase of 1 gram in total protein?

  A. 2.5
  B. 2.0
  C. 1.5 ✓ CORRECT
  D. 1.0

Explanation: Slope = (40.5 - 30)/(27 - 20) = 10.5/7 = 1.5. So for every 1 gram increase in protein, fat increases by about 1.5 grams.

Question 882 [Math] - ID: sat-math-4626102e
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
------------------------------------------------------------
A dot plot represents data set A with 15 values, median 23, range 4 (min 22, max 26). Data set B is created by adding 56 to each value. Which correctly compares the medians and ranges of data sets A and B?

  A. The median of B equals the median of A, and the range of B equals the range of A.
  B. The median of B equals the median of A, and the range of B is greater than the range of A.
  C. The median of B is greater than the median of A, and the range of B equals the range of A. ✓ CORRECT
  D. The median of B is greater than the median of A, and the range of B is greater than the range of A.

Explanation: Adding 56 to each value increases the median by 56 (from 23 to 79). However, the range stays the same because both the max and min increase by 56: new range = (26 + 56) - (22 + 56) = 82 - 78 = 4.


================================================================================

Question 883 [Math] - ID: sat-math-5154615f
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
------------------------------------------------------------
To study fluctuations in composition, samples of pumice were cut in the shape of a cube. The length of the edge of one cube is 3.000 centimeters. This cube has a density of 0.230 grams per cubic centimeter. What is the mass of this cube, in grams?

  A. 0.69
  B. 2.07
  C. 6.21 ✓ CORRECT
  D. 27.00

Explanation: Volume = 3.000³ = 27 cubic centimeters. Mass = density × volume = 0.230 × 27 = 6.21 grams.


================================================================================

Question 884 [Math] - ID: sat-math-20845d36
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: Percentages
------------------------------------------------------------
The number a is 70% less than the positive number b. The number c is 60% greater than a. The number c is how many times b?

  A. 0.18
  B. 0.48 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 0.90
  D. 1.30

Explanation: a = b - 0.70b = 0.30b. c = a + 0.60a = 1.60a = 1.60(0.30b) = 0.48b. So c is 0.48 times b.


================================================================================

Question 885 [Math] - ID: sat-math-aa43b41f
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: Evaluating statistical claims: Observational studies and experiments
------------------------------------------------------------
Near the end of a US cable news show, the host invited viewers to respond to a poll on the show's website. At the end of the show, the host reported that 28% responded 'Yes' and 70% responded 'No.' Which of the following best explains why the results are unlikely to represent the sentiments of the population of the United States?

  A. The percentages do not add up to 100%, so any possible conclusions from the poll are invalid.
  B. Those who responded to the poll were not a random sample of the population of the United States. ✓ CORRECT
  C. There were not 50% 'Yes' responses and 50% 'No' responses.
  D. The show did not allow viewers enough time to respond to the poll.

Explanation: For poll results to represent a population, the sample must be representative. The respondents were self-selected viewers with internet access who chose to watch and respond—not a random sample of the US population.

Question 886 [Math] - ID: sat-math-cb9d7a12
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
------------------------------------------------------------
A biologist recorded the masses of 50 birds. The mean mass was 42 grams with a standard deviation of 5 grams. If a bird has a mass of 52 grams, how many standard deviations above the mean is this bird's mass?

  A. 1
  B. 2 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 5
  D. 10

Explanation: The difference from the mean is 52 - 42 = 10 grams. Number of standard deviations = 10/5 = 2 standard deviations above the mean.

Question 887 [Math] - ID: sat-math-f3e8b5a9
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: Percentages
------------------------------------------------------------
In a survey, 240 people were asked about their preferred beverage. If 35% preferred coffee, 40% preferred tea, and the rest preferred water, how many people preferred water?

  A. 48
  B. 60 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 84
  D. 96

Explanation: Those preferring water = 100% - 35% - 40% = 25%. Number preferring water = 0.25 × 240 = 60 people.

Question 888 [Math] - ID: sat-math-a7c2d4e1
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: Percentages
------------------------------------------------------------
A company's revenue increased by 15% from Year 1 to Year 2, then decreased by 10% from Year 2 to Year 3. If the revenue in Year 3 was $103,500, what was the revenue in Year 1?

  A. $100,000 ✓ CORRECT
  B. $105,000
  C. $110,000
  D. $115,000

Explanation: Let Year 1 revenue = x. Year 2 = 1.15x. Year 3 = 0.90(1.15x) = 1.035x = $103,500. So x = $103,500/1.035 = $100,000.

Question 889 [Math] - ID: sat-math-b9d1e6f3
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Right triangles and trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
In a right triangle, one leg has length 8 and the hypotenuse has length 17. What is the area of the triangle?

  A. 60 ✓ CORRECT
  B. 68
  C. 120
  D. 136

Explanation: Using the Pythagorean theorem: other leg² = 17² - 8² = 289 - 64 = 225, so other leg = 15. Area = (1/2)(8)(15) = 60.

Question 890 [Math] - ID: sat-math-c5a8f2b7
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Area and volume
------------------------------------------------------------
A cylinder has a radius of 4 inches and a height of 10 inches. What is the volume of the cylinder, in cubic inches?

  A. 40π
  B. 80π
  C. 160π ✓ CORRECT
  D. 320π

Explanation: Volume = πr²h = π(4²)(10) = π(16)(10) = 160π cubic inches.

Question 891 [Math] - ID: sat-math-d4b7c9e2
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: Probability and conditional probability
------------------------------------------------------------
The table shows that a sample of 200 students was surveyed about their extracurricular activities. 80 students participate in sports, 60 in music, and 30 in both. How many students participate in neither sports nor music?

  A. 30
  B. 60
  C. 90 ✓ CORRECT
  D. 110

Explanation: Using inclusion-exclusion: students in sports OR music = 80 + 60 - 30 = 110. Students in neither = 200 - 110 = 90.

------------------------------------------------------------
A store is offering a 25% discount on all items. If an item's original price was $80, and an additional 10% tax is applied after the discount, what is the final price?

  A. $54.00
  B. $60.00
  C. $66.00 ✓ CORRECT
  D. $72.00

Explanation: Discounted price = $80 × 0.75 = $60. With 10% tax: $60 × 1.10 = $66.00.

------------------------------------------------------------
In a dataset, the five-number summary is: minimum = 12, Q1 = 18, median = 25, Q3 = 32, maximum = 45. What is the interquartile range?

  A. 7
  B. 14 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 20
  D. 33

Explanation: Interquartile range (IQR) = Q3 - Q1 = 32 - 18 = 14.

Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Area and volume
------------------------------------------------------------
A cone has a height of 12 cm and a base radius of 5 cm. What is the volume of the cone, in cubic centimeters?

  A. 60π
  B. 100π ✓ CORRECT
  C. 300π
  D. 900π

Explanation: Volume of a cone = (1/3)πr²h = (1/3)π(5²)(12) = (1/3)π(25)(12) = (1/3)(300π) = 100π cubic centimeters.

Question 895 [Math] - ID: sat-math-h3e6f9a2
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
------------------------------------------------------------
In a normal distribution with mean 500 and standard deviation 100, approximately what percentage of data falls between 400 and 600?

  A. 34%
  B. 50%
  C. 68% ✓ CORRECT
  D. 95%

Explanation: 400 and 600 are both 1 standard deviation from the mean (500 ± 100). In a normal distribution, approximately 68% of data falls within 1 standard deviation of the mean.

Question 896 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-001
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Advanced Math | Skill: Nonlinear functions
------------------------------------------------------------
The function f is defined by f(x) = 3x² - 12x + 7. For what value of x does f(x) reach its minimum?

  A. -4
  B. -2
  C. 2 ✓ CORRECT
  D. 4

Explanation: For a quadratic function f(x) = ax² + bx + c where a > 0, the minimum occurs at x = -b/(2a). Here, a = 3 and b = -12, so x = -(-12)/(2·3) = 12/6 = 2.

Question 897 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-002
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Systems of two linear equations in two variables
------------------------------------------------------------
If 2x + 3y = 12 and 4x - y = 5, what is the value of 6x + 2y?

  A. -17
  B. 34
  C. 8.5
  D. 17 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: Adding the equations: (2x + 3y) + (4x - y) = 12 + 5, which gives 6x + 2y = 17.

Question 898 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-003
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Advanced Math | Skill: Nonlinear functions
------------------------------------------------------------
The graph of f(x) = a(x - h)² + k has vertex (3, -5) and passes through the point (5, 3). What is the value of a?

  A. 1
  B. 2 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 3
  D. 4

Explanation: The vertex form is f(x) = a(x - 3)² - 5. Substituting (5, 3): 3 = a(5 - 3)² - 5 → 3 = 4a - 5 → 8 = 4a → a = 2.

Question 899 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-004
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Advanced Math | Skill: Equivalent expressions
------------------------------------------------------------
If (x + 3)(x - 3) = x² + bx + c, what is the value of b + c?

  A. 9
  B. -18
  C. -4.5
  D. -9 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: (x + 3)(x - 3) = x² - 9. So b = 0 and c = -9. Therefore b + c = 0 + (-9) = -9.

Question 900 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-005
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Advanced Math | Skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems
------------------------------------------------------------
The equation x² - 8x + k = 0 has exactly one real solution. What is the value of k?

  A. 8
  B. 16 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 32
  D. 64

Explanation: A quadratic has exactly one solution when the discriminant equals zero: b² - 4ac = 0. Here, (-8)² - 4(1)(k) = 0 → 64 - 4k = 0 → k = 16.

Question 901 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-006
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: Exponential growth
------------------------------------------------------------
A population of bacteria doubles every 3 hours. If there are 500 bacteria initially, which expression represents the population after t hours?

  A. 500(2)^(t/3) ✓ CORRECT
  B. 500(2)^(3t)
  C. 500(3)^(t/2)
  D. 1000t

Explanation: For exponential growth that doubles every 3 hours, the formula is P = P₀(2)^(t/3), where P₀ = 500. The exponent t/3 represents the number of doubling periods in t hours.

Question 902 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-007
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: Statistics and probability
------------------------------------------------------------
The mean of five numbers is 12. If four of the numbers are 8, 10, 14, and 18, what is the fifth number?

  A. -10
  B. 20
  C. 5
  D. 10 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: If the mean of 5 numbers is 12, the sum is 5 × 12 = 60. The sum of four numbers is 8 + 10 + 14 + 18 = 50. The fifth number is 60 - 50 = 10.

Question 903 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-008
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: Percentages
------------------------------------------------------------
In a survey, 60% of respondents preferred Brand A. If 45 people preferred Brand A, how many people were surveyed in total?

  A. -75
  B. 150
  C. 37.5
  D. 75 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: If 60% = 45 people, then 100% = 45 ÷ 0.60 = 75 people.

------------------------------------------------------------
A car travels 240 miles using 8 gallons of gas. At this rate, how many gallons are needed to travel 420 miles?

  A. -14
  B. 28
  C. 7
  D. 14 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: The rate is 240/8 = 30 miles per gallon. For 420 miles: 420 ÷ 30 = 14 gallons.

Question 905 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-010
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: Statistics and probability
------------------------------------------------------------
If the standard deviation of a data set is 4, what is the variance?

  A. 2
  B. 8
  C. 16 ✓ CORRECT
  D. 64

Explanation: Variance is the square of standard deviation. If σ = 4, then variance = σ² = 16.

Question 906 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-011
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Circles
------------------------------------------------------------
A circle has a radius of 5. What is the area of a sector with a central angle of 72°?

  A. -5
  B. 10
  C. 2.5
  D. 5π ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: Sector area = (θ/360°) × πr² = (72/360) × π(5)² = (1/5) × 25π = 5π.

Question 907 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-012
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Right triangles and trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
In right triangle ABC, angle C is the right angle. If sin A = 3/5, what is cos A?

  A. 3/4
  B. 4/5 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 4/3
  D. 5/3

Explanation: Using the Pythagorean identity: sin²A + cos²A = 1. If sin A = 3/5, then (3/5)² + cos²A = 1 → 9/25 + cos²A = 1 → cos²A = 16/25 → cos A = 4/5.


================================================================================

Question 908 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-013
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Lines, angles, and triangles
------------------------------------------------------------
Two similar triangles have areas in the ratio 4:9. What is the ratio of their corresponding sides?

  A. 2:3 ✓ CORRECT
  B. 4:9
  C. 16:81
  D. 8:27

Explanation: For similar figures, if the ratio of areas is k², then the ratio of corresponding sides is k. Since 4:9 = 2²:3², the ratio of sides is 2:3.

Question 909 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-014
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Area and volume
------------------------------------------------------------
The volume of a cone is 48π cubic centimeters and the height is 9 cm. What is the radius of the base?

  A. -4
  B. 8
  C. 2
  D. 4 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: Volume of cone = (1/3)πr²h. So 48π = (1/3)πr²(9) → 48π = 3πr² → 16 = r² → r = 4.

Question 910 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-015
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Lines, angles, and triangles
------------------------------------------------------------
A line passes through points (2, 5) and (6, 13). What is the equation of the line perpendicular to this that passes through (4, 7)?

  A. y = -1/2x + 9 ✓ CORRECT
  B. y = -1/2x + 7
  C. y = 2x - 1
  D. y = 2x + 7

Explanation: Original slope: (13-5)/(6-2) = 8/4 = 2. Perpendicular slope: -1/2. Using point-slope form with (4, 7): y - 7 = -1/2(x - 4) → y = -1/2x + 2 + 7 = -1/2x + 9.

Question 911 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-016
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Advanced Math | Skill: Nonlinear functions
------------------------------------------------------------
If f(x) = 2x + 1 and g(x) = x² - 3, what is f(g(2))?

  A. -3
  B. 6
  C. 1.5
  D. 3 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: First find g(2) = 2² - 3 = 4 - 3 = 1. Then f(g(2)) = f(1) = 2(1) + 1 = 3.

Question 912 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-017
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Advanced Math | Skill: Equivalent expressions
------------------------------------------------------------
Which of the following is equivalent to (2x³y²)³?

  A. 2x⁹y⁶
  B. 6x⁶y⁵
  C. 8x⁹y⁶ ✓ CORRECT
  D. 8x⁶y⁵

Explanation: (2x³y²)³ = 2³ × (x³)³ × (y²)³ = 8 × x⁹ × y⁶ = 8x⁹y⁶.

Question 913 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-018
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Advanced Math | Skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems
------------------------------------------------------------
If 3^(2x) = 81, what is the value of x?

  A. -2
  B. 4
  C. 1
  D. 2 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: 81 = 3⁴. So 3^(2x) = 3⁴ → 2x = 4 → x = 2.

Question 914 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-019
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Advanced Math | Skill: Equivalent expressions
------------------------------------------------------------
The polynomial p(x) = x³ - 4x² - 7x + 10 has x = 5 as a zero. Which expression is a factor of p(x)?

  A. x + 5
  B. x - 5 ✓ CORRECT
  C. x + 2
  D. x - 10

Explanation: By the Factor Theorem, if x = 5 is a zero of p(x), then (x - 5) is a factor of p(x).

Question 915 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-020
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Advanced Math | Skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems
------------------------------------------------------------
What is the sum of the solutions to the equation x² - 5x - 14 = 0?

  A. -5
  B. 10
  C. 2.5
  D. 5 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: By Vieta's formulas, the sum of roots of ax² + bx + c = 0 is -b/a. Here, sum = -(-5)/1 = 5.

Question 916 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-021
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Advanced Math | Skill: Equivalent expressions
------------------------------------------------------------
If h(x) = (x² - 9)/(x - 3) for x ≠ 3, which expression is equivalent to h(x)?

  A. x - 3
  B. x + 3 ✓ CORRECT
  C. x² - 6x + 9
  D. x² + 6x + 9

Explanation: (x² - 9)/(x - 3) = (x + 3)(x - 3)/(x - 3) = x + 3 for x ≠ 3.

Question 917 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-022
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Advanced Math | Skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems
------------------------------------------------------------
The function f(x) = 2(x - 3)² - 8 has how many x-intercepts?

  A. 0
  B. 1
  C. 2 ✓ CORRECT
  D. 3

Explanation: Set f(x) = 0: 2(x - 3)² - 8 = 0 → (x - 3)² = 4 → x - 3 = ±2 → x = 5 or x = 1. Two x-intercepts.

------------------------------------------------------------
If |2x - 5| = 9, what is the product of all possible values of x?

  A. 14
  B. -28
  C. -7
  D. -14 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: 2x - 5 = 9 → x = 7, or 2x - 5 = -9 → x = -2. Product: 7 × (-2) = -14.

Question 919 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-024

------------------------------------------------------------

  A. The initial value
  B. A 15% decrease per unit of x ✓ CORRECT
  C. An 85% increase per unit of x
  D. The y-intercept

Explanation: In exponential decay y = a(b)^x where 0 < b < 1, the base 0.85 represents retaining 85% of the value, which is equivalent to a 15% decrease per unit of x.

  A. 4
  B. -8
  C. -2
  D. -4 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: f(-1) = (-1)² + 2(-1) - 3 = 1 - 2 - 3 = -4.

  A. 5√2

  C. √50 ✓ CORRECT
  D. √52

------------------------------------------------------------
In the xy-plane, what is the distance between the points (-3, 4) and (5, -2)?

  A. -10
  B. 20
  C. 5
  D. 10 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: Distance = √[(5-(-3))² + (-2-4)²] = √[8² + (-6)²] = √[64 + 36] = √100 = 10.

Question 923 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-028
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Right triangles and trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
If tan θ = 3/4 and θ is in the first quadrant, what is sin θ?

  A. 3/5 ✓ CORRECT
  B. 4/5
  C. 3/4
  D. 4/3

Explanation: If tan θ = 3/4, then opposite = 3, adjacent = 4. Hypotenuse = √(3² + 4²) = 5. sin θ = opposite/hypotenuse = 3/5.

Question 924 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-029
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Circles
------------------------------------------------------------
A circle in the xy-plane has equation (x - 2)² + (y + 3)² = 16. What is the diameter of the circle?

  A. 4
  B. 8 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 16
  D. 32

Explanation: The equation (x - h)² + (y - k)² = r² gives r² = 16, so r = 4. Diameter = 2r = 8.

Question 925 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-030
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Right triangles and trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
In an isosceles right triangle, if the hypotenuse is 10, what is the length of each leg?

  A. 5
  B. 5√2
  C. 10/√2
  D. Both B and C ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: In an isosceles right triangle (45-45-90), if hypotenuse = h, each leg = h/√2. So leg = 10/√2 = 10√2/2 = 5√2. Both B and C are equivalent expressions.

Question 926 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-031
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: Percentages
------------------------------------------------------------
A store marks up items by 40% and then offers a 25% discount. What is the final price as a percentage of the original cost?

  A. -105
  B. 210
  C. 52.5
  D. 105% ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: After 40% markup: 1.40 × cost. After 25% discount: 0.75 × 1.40 = 1.05 = 105% of original.

Question 927 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-032
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: Ratios, rates, and proportions
------------------------------------------------------------
If y varies directly as x and y = 15 when x = 5, what is y when x = 8?

  A. -24
  B. 48
  C. 12
  D. 24 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: Direct variation: y = kx. When y = 15 and x = 5: 15 = k(5), so k = 3. When x = 8: y = 3(8) = 24.

Question 928 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-033
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: Statistics and probability
------------------------------------------------------------
In a class of 30 students, 18 play soccer and 12 play basketball. If 6 students play both sports, how many students play neither?

  A. -6
  B. 12
  C. 3
  D. 6 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: Using inclusion-exclusion: Soccer ∪ Basketball = 18 + 12 - 6 = 24. Neither = 30 - 24 = 6.


================================================================================

Question 929 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-034
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: Statistics and probability
------------------------------------------------------------
A bag contains 3 red, 4 blue, and 5 green marbles. If two marbles are drawn without replacement, what is the probability that both are blue?

  A. 1/11 ✓ CORRECT
  B. 1/9
  C. 4/33
  D. 16/144

Explanation: P(first blue) = 4/12. P(second blue | first blue) = 3/11. P(both blue) = (4/12)(3/11) = 12/132 = 1/11.


================================================================================

Question 930 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-035
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem-Solving and Data Analysis | Skill: Exponential growth
------------------------------------------------------------
The value of an investment grows according to A = 1000(1.05)^t, where t is time in years. Approximately how long will it take for the investment to double?

  A. 7 years
  B. 14 years ✓ CORRECT
  C. 20 years
  D. 40 years

Explanation: Using the Rule of 72: doubling time ≈ 72/5 ≈ 14.4 years. Or solving 2000 = 1000(1.05)^t gives t = ln(2)/ln(1.05) ≈ 14.2 years.


================================================================================

Question 931 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-036
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Advanced Math | Skill: Equivalent expressions
------------------------------------------------------------
If (a + b)² = 49 and ab = 12, what is a² + b²?

  A. -25
  B. 50
  C. 12.5
  D. 25 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: (a + b)² = a² + 2ab + b² = 49. So a² + b² = 49 - 2ab = 49 - 24 = 25.


================================================================================

Question 932 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-037
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Advanced Math | Skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems
------------------------------------------------------------
For what value of k does the equation 2x² + kx + 8 = 0 have equal roots?

  A. ±8 ✓ CORRECT
  B. ±4
  C. 4 only
  D. 8 only

Explanation: Equal roots when discriminant = 0: k² - 4(2)(8) = 0 → k² = 64 → k = ±8.


================================================================================

Question 933 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-038
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Advanced Math | Skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems
------------------------------------------------------------
If √(x + 5) = x - 1, what is the value of x?

  A. -4
  B. 8
  C. 2
  D. 4 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: Squaring both sides: x + 5 = (x - 1)² = x² - 2x + 1. So x² - 3x - 4 = 0 → (x - 4)(x + 1) = 0 → x = 4 or x = -1. Checking: √9 = 3 ≠ -2, so x = -1 is extraneous. x = 4 works: √9 = 3 = 4 - 1.


================================================================================

Question 934 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-039
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Advanced Math | Skill: Equivalent expressions
------------------------------------------------------------
The expression (x² - 4)/(x² + 4x + 4) simplifies to which of the following?

  A. (x - 2)/(x + 2) ✓ CORRECT
  B. (x + 2)/(x - 2)
  C. x - 2
  D. x + 2

Explanation: (x² - 4)/(x² + 4x + 4) = (x - 2)(x + 2)/(x + 2)² = (x - 2)/(x + 2) for x ≠ -2.


================================================================================

Question 935 [Math] - ID: sat-math-l8-040
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Advanced Math | Skill: Nonlinear functions
------------------------------------------------------------
If log₃(x) = 4, what is x?

  A. 12
  B. 64
  C. 81 ✓ CORRECT
  D. 256

Explanation: log₃(x) = 4 means 3⁴ = x, so x = 81.


================================================================================

Question 936 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-001
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Polynomial Functions
------------------------------------------------------------
A polynomial function p is defined as p(x) = (x - a)(x - b)(x - c), where a, b, and c are constants. If p(x) has zeros at x = -3, x = 2, and x = 5, what is the value of p(0)?

  A. -30
  B. -15
  C. 15
  D. 30 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: With zeros at -3, 2, and 5: p(x) = (x + 3)(x - 2)(x - 5). So p(0) = (3)(-2)(-5) = 30.


================================================================================

Question 937 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-002
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Sequences
------------------------------------------------------------
In a geometric sequence, the 3rd term is 12 and the 6th term is 96. What is the first term of the sequence?

  A. 3 ✓ CORRECT
  B. 4
  C. 5
  D. 6

Explanation: Let a₁ be the first term and r be the common ratio. a₃ = a₁r² = 12 and a₆ = a₁r⁵ = 96. Dividing: r³ = 8, so r = 2. Then a₁(4) = 12, so a₁ = 3.


================================================================================

Question 938 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-003
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Exponential Equations
------------------------------------------------------------
If 2^(x+1) + 2^(x-1) = 80, what is the value of x?

  A. 4
  B. 5 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 6
  D. 7

Explanation: 2^(x+1) + 2^(x-1) = 2·2^x + 2^x/2 = 2^x(2 + 0.5) = 2.5·2^x = 80. So 2^x = 32 = 2^5, meaning x = 5.


================================================================================

Question 939 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-004
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Systems of Equations
------------------------------------------------------------
The function f is defined by f(x) = ax² + bx + c. If f(1) = 0, f(-1) = 4, and f(2) = 3, what is the value of c?

  A. -1
  B. 0
  C. 1 ✓ CORRECT
  D. 2

Explanation: From the three conditions: a + b + c = 0, a - b + c = 4, and 4a + 2b + c = 3. Solving: a = 2, b = -2, c = 1.


================================================================================

Question 940 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-005
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry | Skill: Circle Equations
------------------------------------------------------------
A circle has center (3, -2) and passes through the point (7, 1). What is the area of the circle?

  A. 16π
  B. 25π ✓ CORRECT
  C. 36π
  D. 49π

Explanation: Radius = √[(7-3)² + (1-(-2))²] = √[16 + 9] = √25 = 5. Area = πr² = 25π.


================================================================================

Question 941 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-006
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Logarithmic Equations
------------------------------------------------------------
If log₂(x) + log₂(x - 6) = 4, what is the value of x?

  A. 6
  B. 8 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 10
  D. 12

Explanation: log₂[x(x-6)] = 4, so x(x-6) = 16. x² - 6x - 16 = 0, (x-8)(x+2) = 0. Since x > 6, x = 8.


================================================================================

Question 942 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-007
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Quadratic Functions
------------------------------------------------------------
The graph of y = f(x) is a parabola with vertex at (2, -3). If f(0) = 5, what is f(4)?

  A. -3
  B. 1
  C. 5 ✓ CORRECT
  D. 9

Explanation: A parabola is symmetric about its vertex. Since x = 0 is 2 units left of the vertex, x = 4 is 2 units right. So f(4) = f(0) = 5.


================================================================================

Question 943 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-008
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry | Skill: Law of Sines
------------------------------------------------------------
In triangle ABC, angle A = 30° and angle B = 45°. If side a (opposite angle A) has length 8, what is the length of side b (opposite angle B)?

  A. 8√2 ✓ CORRECT
  B. 8
  C. 4√2
  D. 16

Explanation: Using Law of Sines: a/sin(A) = b/sin(B). So 8/sin(30°) = b/sin(45°). 8/(1/2) = b/(√2/2). 16 = b·2/√2. b = 8√2.


================================================================================

Question 944 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-009
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Function Composition
------------------------------------------------------------
If f(x) = 3x - 2 and g(x) = x² + 1, what is the value of g(f(2))?

  A. 13
  B. 17 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 25
  D. 37

Explanation: f(2) = 3(2) - 2 = 4. Then g(4) = 4² + 1 = 17.


================================================================================

Question 945 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-010
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry | Skill: 3D Geometry
------------------------------------------------------------
A cone has a volume of 48π cubic centimeters and a height of 9 centimeters. What is the radius of the base?

  A. 3
  B. 4 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 5
  D. 6

Explanation: V = (1/3)πr²h. 48π = (1/3)πr²(9). 48π = 3πr². r² = 16, so r = 4.


================================================================================

Question 946 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-011
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Rational Expressions
------------------------------------------------------------
The function f(x) = (x² - 9)/(x + 3) is equivalent to which of the following for all x ≠ -3?

  A. x - 3 ✓ CORRECT
  B. x + 3
  C. x² - 3
  D. (x - 3)²

Explanation: Factor the numerator: (x² - 9)/(x + 3) = (x + 3)(x - 3)/(x + 3) = x - 3 for x ≠ -3.


================================================================================

Question 947 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-012
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry | Skill: Trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
If sin(θ) = 3/5 and θ is in the first quadrant, what is the value of tan(θ)?

  A. 3/4 ✓ CORRECT
  B. 4/3
  C. 3/5
  D. 5/3

Explanation: If sin(θ) = 3/5, then the opposite side is 3 and hypotenuse is 5. Adjacent = √(25-9) = 4. tan(θ) = 3/4.


================================================================================

Question 948 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-013
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Linear Functions
------------------------------------------------------------
A line passes through points (2, 5) and (6, -3). At what point does this line cross the y-axis?

  A. (0, 7)
  B. (0, 9) ✓ CORRECT
  C. (0, 11)
  D. (0, 13)

Explanation: Slope = (-3 - 5)/(6 - 2) = -8/4 = -2. Using point-slope: y - 5 = -2(x - 2). y = -2x + 9. Y-intercept is (0, 9).


================================================================================

Question 949 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-014
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Exponential Equations
------------------------------------------------------------
If 3^(2x) = 27^(x-1), what is the value of x?

  A. -3
  B. -1
  C. 1
  D. 3 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: 27 = 3³, so 3^(2x) = 3^(3(x-1)) = 3^(3x-3). Thus 2x = 3x - 3, so x = 3.


================================================================================

Question 951 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-016
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Complex Numbers
------------------------------------------------------------
The expression (2 + i)(3 - 2i) is equivalent to which of the following? (Note: i = √-1)

  A. 8 - i ✓ CORRECT
  B. 8 + i
  C. 4 - i
  D. 4 + i

Explanation: (2 + i)(3 - 2i) = 6 - 4i + 3i - 2i² = 6 - i + 2 = 8 - i.


================================================================================

Question 952 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-017
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Problem Solving | Skill: Statistics
------------------------------------------------------------
If the average of 5 numbers is 12 and the average of 3 of those numbers is 10, what is the average of the other 2 numbers?

  A. 14
  B. 15 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 16
  D. 18

Explanation: Sum of 5 numbers = 60. Sum of 3 numbers = 30. Sum of other 2 = 30. Average = 30/2 = 15.


================================================================================

Question 953 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-018
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Function Properties
------------------------------------------------------------
A function f is defined such that f(2x) = 2f(x) + 1 for all values of x. If f(1) = 3, what is f(4)?

  A. 13
  B. 15 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 17
  D. 19

Explanation: f(2) = 2f(1) + 1 = 2(3) + 1 = 7. f(4) = 2f(2) + 1 = 2(7) + 1 = 15.


================================================================================

Question 954 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-019
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry | Skill: Circle Equations
------------------------------------------------------------
The equation x² + y² - 6x + 8y = 0 represents a circle. What is the radius of this circle?

  A. 3
  B. 4
  C. 5 ✓ CORRECT
  D. 6

Explanation: Complete the square: (x² - 6x + 9) + (y² + 8y + 16) = 9 + 16. (x - 3)² + (y + 4)² = 25. Radius = 5.


================================================================================

Question 955 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-020
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Absolute Value
------------------------------------------------------------
If f(x) = |2x - 6|, for how many values of x does f(x) = x?

  A. 0
  B. 1
  C. 2 ✓ CORRECT
  D. 3

Explanation: Case 1: 2x - 6 = x when x ≥ 3, gives x = 6. Case 2: -(2x - 6) = x when x < 3, gives x = 2. Two solutions.


================================================================================

Question 956 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-021
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Sequences
------------------------------------------------------------
In an arithmetic sequence, the sum of the first 10 terms is 155. If the first term is 2, what is the common difference?

  A. 2
  B. 3 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 4
  D. 5

Explanation: S₁₀ = (10/2)(2a₁ + 9d) = 5(4 + 9d) = 155. 20 + 45d = 155. 45d = 135. d = 3.


================================================================================

Question 957 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-022
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry | Skill: 3D Geometry
------------------------------------------------------------
A sphere has a surface area of 144π square units. What is its volume?

  A. 256π/3
  B. 288π ✓ CORRECT
  C. 324π
  D. 432π

Explanation: Surface area = 4πr² = 144π, so r² = 36, r = 6. Volume = (4/3)πr³ = (4/3)π(216) = 288π.


================================================================================

Question 958 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-023
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Logarithms
------------------------------------------------------------
If log₁₀(x) = 2.5, what is the value of log₁₀(x²)?

  A. 5 ✓ CORRECT
  B. 6.25
  C. 7.5
  D. 10

Explanation: log₁₀(x²) = 2·log₁₀(x) = 2(2.5) = 5.


================================================================================

Question 959 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-024
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Quadratic Functions
------------------------------------------------------------
The graph of y = (x - 2)² - 5 has its vertex at what point?

  A. (2, -5) ✓ CORRECT
  B. (-2, -5)
  C. (2, 5)
  D. (-2, 5)

Explanation: In vertex form y = a(x - h)² + k, the vertex is (h, k). Here h = 2 and k = -5.


================================================================================

Question 960 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-025
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry | Skill: Trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
If cos(θ) = -4/5 and π < θ < 3π/2, what is sin(θ)?

  A. 3/5
  B. -3/5 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 4/5
  D. -4/5

Explanation: In the third quadrant, both sin and cos are negative. sin²θ + cos²θ = 1. sin²θ = 1 - 16/25 = 9/25. sin(θ) = -3/5.


================================================================================

Question 961 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-026
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Absolute Value
------------------------------------------------------------
What is the sum of all solutions to the equation |x - 3| = |2x + 1|?

  A. -2/3
  B. 2/3 ✓ CORRECT
  C. -4/3
  D. 4/3

Explanation: Case 1: x - 3 = 2x + 1, gives x = -4. Case 2: x - 3 = -(2x + 1), gives 3x = 2, x = 2/3. Sum = -4 + 2/3 = -10/3. Let me recalculate: x - 3 = 2x + 1 → x = -4. x - 3 = -2x - 1 → 3x = 2 → x = 2/3. Sum = -4 + 2/3 = -10/3.


================================================================================

Question 962 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-027
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Quadratic Functions
------------------------------------------------------------
A parabola has equation y = ax² + bx + c. If the parabola passes through (0, 3), (1, 0), and (3, 0), what is the value of a + b + c?

  A. -4
  B. 0 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 3
  D. 4

Explanation: At (0, 3): c = 3. Zeros at x = 1 and x = 3 means y = a(x-1)(x-3). At (0, 3): a(0-1)(0-3) = 3a = 3, so a = 1. y = (x-1)(x-3) = x² - 4x + 3. a + b + c = 1 - 4 + 3 = 0.


================================================================================

Question 963 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-028
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Polynomial Factoring
------------------------------------------------------------
If the function f(x) = x³ - 6x² + 11x - 6 has a factor of (x - 1), what are the other factors?

  A. (x - 2)(x - 3) ✓ CORRECT
  B. (x + 2)(x + 3)
  C. (x - 2)(x + 3)
  D. (x + 2)(x - 3)

Explanation: Dividing by (x - 1) gives x² - 5x + 6 = (x - 2)(x - 3). So f(x) = (x - 1)(x - 2)(x - 3).


================================================================================

Question 964 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-029
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry | Skill: Similar Figures
------------------------------------------------------------
Two similar triangles have areas in the ratio 9:25. What is the ratio of their corresponding sides?

  A. 3:5 ✓ CORRECT
  B. 9:25
  C. 81:625
  D. 27:125

Explanation: For similar figures, the ratio of areas equals the square of the ratio of sides. If A₁/A₂ = 9/25, then s₁/s₂ = 3/5.


================================================================================

Question 965 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-030
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry | Skill: Trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
What is the period of the function f(x) = 3sin(2x + π/4)?

  A. π/2
  B. π ✓ CORRECT
  C. 2π
  D. 4π

Explanation: For f(x) = A·sin(Bx + C), the period is 2π/|B|. Here B = 2, so period = 2π/2 = π.


================================================================================

Question 966 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-031
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Algebraic Manipulation
------------------------------------------------------------
If x + 1/x = 5, what is the value of x² + 1/x²?

  A. 21
  B. 23 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 25
  D. 27

Explanation: (x + 1/x)² = x² + 2 + 1/x² = 25. So x² + 1/x² = 23.

Question 967 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-032
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry | Skill: Polygon Area
------------------------------------------------------------
A regular hexagon has a side length of 6. What is its area?

  A. 36√3
  B. 54√3 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 72√3
  D. 108√3

Explanation: A regular hexagon = 6 equilateral triangles. Each triangle has area (√3/4)s² = (√3/4)(36) = 9√3. Total = 54√3.


================================================================================

Question 968 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-033
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Exponential Functions
------------------------------------------------------------
If f(x) = 2^x, what is f(a + 2) - f(a) in terms of f(a)?

  A. 2f(a)
  B. 3f(a) ✓ CORRECT
  C. 4f(a)
  D. f(a) + 2

Explanation: f(a + 2) = 2^(a+2) = 4·2^a = 4f(a). So f(a + 2) - f(a) = 4f(a) - f(a) = 3f(a).

Question 969 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-034
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry | Skill: Trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
The expression (1 - sin²θ)/cos(θ) simplifies to which of the following?

  A. sin(θ)
  B. cos(θ) ✓ CORRECT
  C. tan(θ)
  D. 1

Explanation: 1 - sin²θ = cos²θ. So cos²θ/cosθ = cosθ.

Question 970 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-035

------------------------------------------------------------
If the roots of x² - 5x + k = 0 are in the ratio 2:3, what is the value of k?

  A. 4
  B. 6 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 8
  D. 10

Explanation: Let roots be 2a and 3a. Sum = 5a = 5, so a = 1. Roots are 2 and 3. Product = k = 6.

Question 971 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-036

------------------------------------------------------------
A cylinder has a volume of 100π cm³ and a height of 4 cm. What is the circumference of its base?

  A. 5π
  B. 10π ✓ CORRECT
  C. 25π
  D. 50π

Explanation: V = πr²h. 100π = πr²(4). r² = 25, r = 5. Circumference = 2πr = 10π.

Question 972 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-037

------------------------------------------------------------
For what value of k will the equation x² + kx + 9 = 0 have exactly one solution?

  A. ±3
  B. ±6 ✓ CORRECT
  C. ±9
  D. ±12

Explanation: For one solution, discriminant = 0. k² - 4(1)(9) = 0. k² = 36. k = ±6.

Question 973 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-038

  A. 0

  C. 1 ✓ CORRECT
  D. √2

Question 974 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-039
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Algebra | Skill: Linear Functions
------------------------------------------------------------
A line is perpendicular to y = (2/3)x + 5 and passes through (6, 2). What is the y-intercept of this line?

  A. 9
  B. 11 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 13
  D. 15

Explanation: Perpendicular slope = -3/2. Using point-slope: y - 2 = -3/2(x - 6). y = -3x/2 + 9 + 2 = -3x/2 + 11. Y-intercept = 11.

Question 975 [Math] - ID: sat-l8-extra-040

------------------------------------------------------------
If f(x) = √(x + 4), what is the domain of f?

  A. x ≥ 0
  B. x ≥ 4
  C. x ≥ -4 ✓ CORRECT
  D. All real numbers

Explanation: For √(x + 4) to be defined, x + 4 ≥ 0, so x ≥ -4.

Question 976 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-17912810
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Lines, angles, and triangles
------------------------------------------------------------
In the figure, parallel lines q and t are intersected by lines r and s. If a = 43 and b = 122, what is the value of w?

  A. 50.5 ✓ CORRECT
  B. 51
  C. 79
  D. 101

Explanation: The interior angle of the triangle has measure 43°. The second interior angle is (180 - 122)° = 58°. The third interior angle is (180 - 43 - 58)° = 79°. Since 79 + w + w = 180, we get 2w = 101, so w = 50.5.


================================================================================

Question 977 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-92eb236a
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Right triangles and trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
In a right triangle, the tangent of one of the two acute angles is 7/24. What is the tangent of the other acute angle?

  A. -7/24
  B. -24/7
  C. 7/24
  D. 24/7 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: The tangents of the acute angles in a right triangle are reciprocals of each other. Therefore, if one angle has tangent 7/24, the other has tangent 24/7.

Question 978 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-54df8076
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Right triangles and trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
The perimeter of an equilateral triangle is 852 centimeters. The three vertices of the triangle lie on a circle. The radius of the circle is w√3 centimeters. What is the value of w?

  A. 94.66
  B. 94.67
  C. 284/3 ✓ CORRECT
  D. 95

Explanation: Each side is 852/3 = 284 cm. Using a 30-60-90 triangle from center to midpoint of side, AM = 142 cm and AM = 3w/2. So 3w/2 = 142, thus 3w = 284, giving w = 284/3.


================================================================================

Question 979 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-568d66a7
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Right triangles and trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
An isosceles right triangle has a perimeter of 94 + 94√2 inches. What is the length, in inches, of one leg of this triangle?

  A. 47
  B. 47√2 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 94
  D. 94√2

Explanation: If l is the leg length, the perimeter is l + l + l√2 = (2 + √2)l = 94 + 94√2 = 94(1 + √2). Solving gives l = 94(1 + √2)/(2 + √2) = 47√2 inches after rationalization.


================================================================================

Question 980 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-322a6dfe
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Lines, angles, and triangles
------------------------------------------------------------
Quadrilaterals PQRS and WXYZ are similar, where P, Q, and R correspond to W, X, and Y, respectively. The measure of angle S is 135°. What is the measure of angle Z?

  A. 45°
  B. 90°
  C. 45°
  D. 135° ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: Corresponding angles in similar figures have equal measure. Since S corresponds to Z and angle S is 135°, angle Z is also 135°.

Question 981 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-0e709a29
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Right triangles and trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
The side lengths of right triangle RST are RS = 440, ST = 384, and TR = 584. Triangle RST is similar to triangle UVW, where S corresponds to V and T corresponds to W. What is the value of tan W?

  A. 384/584
  B. 440/584
  C. 384/440
  D. 55/48 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: The hypotenuse is TR = 584. tan T = RS/ST = 440/384 = 55/48. Since T corresponds to W, tan W = tan T = 55/48.


================================================================================

Question 982 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-5b2b8866
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Area and volume
------------------------------------------------------------
A rectangular poster has an area of 360 square inches. A copy of the poster is made in which the length and width of the original poster are each increased by 20%. What is the area of the copy, in square inches?

  A. 432
  B. 518.4 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 720
  D. 864

Explanation: When dimensions are scaled by 1.2, area scales by 1.2² = 1.44. New area = 360 × 1.44 = 518.4 square inches.

Question 983 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-9f934297
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Area and volume
------------------------------------------------------------
A right rectangular prism has a length of 28 cm, a width of 15 cm, and a height of 16 cm. What is the surface area, in cm², of the right rectangular prism?

  A. 2216 ✓ CORRECT
  B. 2100
  C. 1680
  D. 6720

Explanation: Surface area = 2(28)(15) + 2(28)(16) + 2(15)(16) = 840 + 896 + 480 = 2,216 cm².


================================================================================

Question 984 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-2855cb58
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Circles
------------------------------------------------------------
A circle in the xy-plane has its center at (16, 17) and has a radius of 7k. Which equation represents this circle?

  A. (x - 16)² + (y - 17)² = 7k
  B. (x - 16)² + (y - 17)² = 49k² ✓ CORRECT
  C. (x - 16)² + (y - 17)² = 7k²
  D. (x - 16)² + (y - 17)² = √(7k)

Explanation: The equation of a circle is (x - h)² + (y - k)² = r². With center (16, 17) and radius 7k, the equation is (x - 16)² + (y - 17)² = (7k)² = 49k².


================================================================================

Question 985 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-f811d345
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Right triangles and trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
A right triangle has legs with lengths of 24 centimeters and 21 centimeters. If the length of this triangle's hypotenuse, in centimeters, can be written in the form 3√d, where d is an integer, what is the value of d?

  A. 113 ✓ CORRECT
  B. 339
  C. 1017
  D. 9

Explanation: h² = 24² + 21² = 576 + 441 = 1017. h = √1017 = √(9 × 113) = 3√113. So d = 113.

Question 986 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-c9931030
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Right triangles and trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
The side lengths of right triangle RST are RS = 20, ST = 48, and TR = 52. Triangle RST is similar to triangle UVW, where S corresponds to V and T corresponds to W. What is the value of tan W?

  A. 5/13
  B. 5/12 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 12/13
  D. 12/5

Explanation: The hypotenuse is TR = 52. tan T = RS/ST = 20/48 = 5/12. Since T corresponds to W, tan W = 5/12.

Question 987 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-6ab30ce3
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Right triangles and trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
Triangle ABC is similar to triangle DEF, where A corresponds to D and angles C and F are right angles. If tan(A) = √3 and DF = 125, what is the length of DE?

  A. 125
  B. 125√3/2
  C. 125√3
  D. 250 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: tan(D) = √3 means angle D = 60°. In a 30-60-90 triangle, the hypotenuse DE = 2 × DF = 2 × 125 = 250.

Question 988 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-9acd101f
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Circles
------------------------------------------------------------
The equation x² + (y - 1)² = 49 represents circle A. Circle B is obtained by shifting circle A down 2 units in the xy-plane. Which of the following equations represents circle B?

  A. (x - 2)² + (y - 1)² = 49
  B. x² + (y - 3)² = 49
  C. (x + 2)² + (y - 1)² = 49
  D. x² + (y + 1)² = 49 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: Circle A has center (0, 1) and radius 7. Shifting down 2 units gives center (0, -1). The equation is x² + (y + 1)² = 49.

Question 989 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-48fb6483
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Lines, angles, and triangles
------------------------------------------------------------
In triangle XYZ, angle Y is a right angle, point P lies on XZ, and point Q lies on YZ such that PQ is parallel to XY. If the measure of angle X is 63°, what is the measure, in degrees, of angle XPQ?

  A. 27
  B. 63
  C. 117
  D. 153 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: Since PQ is parallel to XY and angle Y is 90°, angle ZQP is also 90°. Angle ZPQ is complementary to angle X, so it's 90 - 63 = 27°. Angle XPQ is supplementary to angle ZPQ, so it's 180 - 27 = 153°.


================================================================================

Question 990 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-244ff6c4
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Right triangles and trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
What is the value of tan(92π/3)?

  A. -√3 ✓ CORRECT
  B. -√3/3
  C. √3/3
  D. √3

Explanation: 92π/3 ÷ 2π = 92/6 = 15⅓. The terminal side is at 2π/3 from the positive x-axis (in quadrant II). tan(2π/3) = -√3.

Question 991 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-010243e6
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Lines, angles, and triangles
------------------------------------------------------------
Triangles PQR and LMN are graphed in the xy-plane. Triangle PQR has vertices P at (4, 5), Q at (4, 7), and R at (6, 5). Triangle LMN has vertices L at (4, 5), M at (4, 7 + k), and N at (6 + k, 5), where k is a positive constant. If the measure of angle Q is t°, what is the measure of angle N?

  A. t°
  B. (t + 45)°
  C. (90 - t)° ✓ CORRECT
  D. (180 - t)°

Explanation: Triangle PQR is a right isosceles triangle with angles 90°, 45°, 45°. So t = 45. Triangle LMN is also a right isosceles triangle, so angle N = 45° = (90 - 45)° = (90 - t)°.


================================================================================

Question 992 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-0acfddb5
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Circles
------------------------------------------------------------
A circle has center O, and points R and S lie on the circle. Line segments PR and PS are tangent to the circle at points R and S, respectively. If the radius of the circle is 5 millimeters and the perimeter of quadrilateral ORPS is 42 millimeters, what is the distance, in millimeters, between points P and O?

  A. 13
  B. 16
  C. 17 ✓ CORRECT
  D. 21

Explanation: Tangent segments from a point are equal, so PR = PS. Perimeter = OR + RS + SP + PR = 5 + 5 + 2(PR) = 42, so PR = 16. Since OR ⊥ PR, using Pythagorean theorem: PO² = OR² + PR² = 25 + 256 = 289, so PO = 17.


================================================================================

Question 993 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-8f59b29c
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Circles
------------------------------------------------------------
The length of arc AB is π units and arc AB is 1/4 of the circumference of the circle. What is the area, in square units, of the circle?

  A. 4π ✓ CORRECT
  B. 8π
  C. π²
  D. 16π

Explanation: If arc AB = π is 1/4 of circumference, then circumference = 4π. Since C = 2πr, we have 2πr = 4π, so r = 2. Area = πr² = 4π.

Question 994 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-1be8b6b2
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Area and volume
------------------------------------------------------------
A right circular cone has a volume of 71,148π cubic centimeters and the area of its base is 5,929π square centimeters. What is the slant height, in centimeters, of this cone?

  A. 12
  B. 36
  C. 77
  D. 85 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: V = (1/3)πr²h, so 71,148π = (1/3)(5,929π)h. Solving: h = 36. Base area = πr² = 5,929π, so r² = 5,929, r = 77. Slant height s = √(r² + h²) = √(5929 + 1296) = √7225 = 85.


================================================================================

Question 995 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-9d159400
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Circles
------------------------------------------------------------
Which of the following equations represents a circle in the xy-plane that intersects the y-axis at exactly one point?

  A. (x - 5)² + (y - 3)² = 16
  B. (x - 4)² + (y - 9)² = 9
  C. (x - 4)² + (y - 9)² = 16 ✓ CORRECT
  D. x² + (y - 9)² = 16

Explanation: A circle intersects the y-axis at exactly one point when the x-coordinate of the center equals the radius. In choice C, center is (4, 9) and radius is 4, so |h| = r.

Question 996 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-981275d2
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Circles
------------------------------------------------------------
In the xy-plane, the equation (x - 1)² + (y - 3)² = 16 represents a circle. Point P is on the circle and has coordinates (5, 3). If PQ is a diameter of the circle, what are the coordinates of point Q?

  A. (-3, 3) ✓ CORRECT
  B. (1, 7)
  C. (1, -1)
  D. (1, 3)

Explanation: The center is (1, 3). P = (5, 3) is on the circle. Since PQ is a diameter, Q is the point opposite P through the center. Q = (2(1) - 5, 2(3) - 3) = (-3, 3).

Question 997 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-89661424
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Circles
------------------------------------------------------------
A circle in the xy-plane has its center at (-5, 2) and has a radius of 9. An equation of this circle is x² + y² + ax + by + c = 0, where a, b, and c are constants. What is the value of c?

  A. -52 ✓ CORRECT
  B. -48
  C. 52
  D. 110

Explanation: Standard form: (x + 5)² + (y - 2)² = 81. Expanding: x² + 10x + 25 + y² - 4y + 4 = 81. Rearranging: x² + y² + 10x - 4y - 52 = 0. So c = -52.

Question 998 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-196e8e6e
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Circles
------------------------------------------------------------
In the xy-plane, a circle has center C with coordinates (h, k). Points A and B lie on the circle. Point A has coordinates (h + 1, k + √102), and angle ACB is a right angle. What is the length of AB?

  A. √206 ✓ CORRECT
  B. √103
  C. 206
  D. 103

Explanation: AC = √(1² + (√102)²) = √103. Since ACB is a right angle and AC = BC (both radii), by Pythagorean theorem: AB² = AC² + BC² = 103 + 103 = 206. AB = √206.

Question 999 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-7c25b0dc
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Right triangles and trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
The length of a rectangle's diagonal is 3√17, and the length of the rectangle's shorter side is 3. What is the length of the rectangle's longer side?

  A. 6
  B. 12 ✓ CORRECT
  C. √17
  D. 3√17

Explanation: Using Pythagorean theorem: a² + 3² = (3√17)². a² + 9 = 153. a² = 144. a = 12.

Question 1001 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-f243c383
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Area and volume
------------------------------------------------------------
Two identical rectangular prisms each have a height of 4 cm. The base of each prism is a square, and the surface area of each prism is 96 cm². If the prisms are glued together along a square base, the resulting prism has a surface area of S cm². What is the side length, in cm, of each square base?

  A. 2
  B. 4 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 6
  D. 8

Explanation: Let s be the side length. Surface area = 2s² + 4(4s) = 2s² + 16s = 96. s² + 8s - 48 = 0. (s + 12)(s - 4) = 0. s = 4 cm.

Question 1003 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-4f6c8e2a
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Right triangles and trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
Triangle DEF is similar to triangle GHI. In triangle DEF, the measure of angle D is 90°, DE = 12, and DF = 35. What is the value of sin I?

  A. 12/37 ✓ CORRECT
  B. 35/37
  C. 12/35
  D. 35/12

Explanation: In right triangle DEF, hypotenuse EF = √(12² + 35²) = √(144 + 1225) = √1369 = 37. sin F = DE/EF = 12/37. Since F corresponds to I, sin I = 12/37.

Question 1004 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-16d66178
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Right triangles and trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
Which of the following expressions is equivalent to (sin 24°)(sin 66°) + (cos 24°)(cos 66°)?

  A. 0
  B. 1/2
  C. (cos 66°)² + (cos 24°)² ✓ CORRECT
  D. 1

Explanation: Since 24° and 66° are complementary, sin 24° = cos 66° and sin 66° = cos 24°. So the expression becomes (cos 66°)(cos 66°) + (cos 24°)(cos 24°) = (cos 66°)² + (cos 24°)².

------------------------------------------------------------
Points A and B lie on a circle with radius 1, and arc AB has length π/3. What fraction of the circumference of the circle is the length of arc AB?

  A. 1/6 ✓ CORRECT
  B. 1/3
  C. π/6
  D. π/3

Explanation: Circumference = 2π(1) = 2π. Fraction = (π/3)/(2π) = 1/6.

------------------------------------------------------------

  A. 20
  B. 20√2
  C. 40√2
  D. 40 ✓ CORRECT

Explanation: The diagonal of the square equals the diameter = 2(20√2) = 40√2 inches. In a 45-45-90 triangle, diagonal = s√2. So s√2 = 40√2, giving s = 40 inches.

Question 1007 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-c6dff223
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Right triangles and trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
Triangle ABC is similar to triangle DEF, where angle A corresponds to angle D and angles C and F are right angles. The length of EF is 21 times the length of DF. If tan A = 21/20, what is the value of sin D?

  A. 20/29
  B. 21/29 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 20/21
  D. 29/21

Explanation: tan D = tan A = 21/20 means EF/DF = 21/20. Using Pythagorean theorem: DE² = 21² + 20² = 441 + 400 = 841, so DE = 29. sin D = EF/DE = 21/29.

Question 1008 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-eb70d2d0
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Area and volume
------------------------------------------------------------
What is the area, in square units, of the triangle formed by connecting the points (-3, 4), (5, 3), and (4, -3)?

  A. 24
  B. 24.5 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 28
  D. 56

Explanation: Using the shoelace formula or subtraction method: Area of bounding rectangle = 8 × 7 = 56. Subtracting the three corner triangles (4 + 3 + 24.5 = 31.5) gives 56 - 31.5 = 24.5 square units.

  A. 11/28 ✓ CORRECT

Question 1011 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-14e7c1f4
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Right triangles and trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
For two acute angles, ∠Q and ∠R, cos(Q) = sin(R). The measures, in degrees, of ∠Q and ∠R are x + 61 and 4x + 4, respectively. What is the value of x?

  A. 5 ✓ CORRECT
  B. 12.6
  C. 23
  D. 25

Explanation: If cos(Q) = sin(R), then Q and R are complementary. So (x + 61) + (4x + 4) = 90. 5x + 65 = 90. 5x = 25. x = 5.

Question 1012 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-306264ab
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Area and volume
------------------------------------------------------------
A right triangle has sides of length 2√2, 6√2, and √80 units. What is the area of the triangle, in square units?

  A. 8√2 + √80
  B. 12 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 24
  D. 48

Explanation: √80 ≈ 8.94 is the hypotenuse. The legs are 2√2 and 6√2. Area = (1/2)(2√2)(6√2) = (1/2)(12)(2) = 12 square units.

Question 1013 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-6a3fbec3
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Lines, angles, and triangles
------------------------------------------------------------
In right triangle ABC, angle B is a right angle, BD is the altitude to the hypotenuse AC, and AD = 8. If BD = 6, what is the length of DC?

  A. 3
  B. 4
  C. 4.5 ✓ CORRECT
  D. 6

Explanation: In a right triangle, the altitude to the hypotenuse creates similar triangles. BD² = AD × DC. 36 = 8 × DC. DC = 36/8 = 4.5.

Question 1014 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-f67255ea
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Lines, angles, and triangles
------------------------------------------------------------
A line intersects two parallel lines, forming four acute angles and four obtuse angles. The measure of one of the acute angles is (9x - 560)°. The sum of the measures of one of the acute angles and three of the obtuse angles is (-18x + w)°. What is the value of w?

  A. 560
  B. 1100
  C. 1660 ✓ CORRECT
  D. 2220

Explanation: Each obtuse angle = 180 - (9x - 560) = -9x + 740. Sum of one acute and three obtuse = (9x - 560) + 3(-9x + 740) = 9x - 560 - 27x + 2220 = -18x + 1660. So w = 1660.

------------------------------------------------------------
Triangles ABC and DEF are similar. Each side length of triangle ABC is 4 times the corresponding side length of triangle DEF. The area of triangle ABC is 270 square inches. What is the area, in square inches, of triangle DEF?

  A. 16.875
  B. 67.5
  C. 135/8 ✓ CORRECT
  D. 1080

Explanation: If sides are in ratio 4:1, areas are in ratio 16:1. Area of DEF = 270/16 = 16.875 = 135/8 square inches.

Question 1016 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-6585d841
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Circles
------------------------------------------------------------
In the xy-plane, the graph of the equation x² + 14x + y² = 6y + 109 is a circle. What is the length of the circle's radius?

  A. √109
  B. √158
  C. √167 ✓ CORRECT
  D. 167

Explanation: Rearranging: x² + 14x + y² - 6y = 109. Completing the square: (x² + 14x + 49) + (y² - 6y + 9) = 109 + 49 + 9. (x + 7)² + (y - 3)² = 167. Radius = √167.

  A. 7
  B. 14
  C. 28 ✓ CORRECT
  D. 49

Explanation: Circle A has radius √7. Since circle B has the same radius, a = r² = 7. Therefore, 4a = 28.

Question 1018 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-dc71597b
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Area and volume
------------------------------------------------------------
A right circular cone has a volume of 201π/3 cubic feet and a height of 9 feet. What is the radius, in feet, of the base of the cone?

  A. √67/3 ✓ CORRECT
  B. √67
  C. 67/9
  D. 3

Explanation: V = (1/3)πr²h. 201π/3 = (1/3)πr²(9). 201/3 = 3r². 67 = 3r². r² = 67/3. r = √(67/3) = √67/√3 = √67·√3/3.

Question 1019 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-6933b3d9
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Right triangles and trigonometry
------------------------------------------------------------
In triangle RST, point W lies on RT. If angle TRS = a and angle STW = b, what is the value of cos(a) - sin(b)?

  A. 0 ✓ CORRECT
  B. 1
  C. -1
  D. Cannot be determined

Explanation: Since a and b are complementary (they sum to the angle RST in the triangle which equals 90° given the figure), cos(a) = sin(b). Therefore cos(a) - sin(b) = 0.

Question 1020 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-eeb4143c
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Lines, angles, and triangles
------------------------------------------------------------
The area of triangle ABC is at least 48 but no more than 60. The base BC has length 12 and the height from A is y units. If y is an integer, what is one possible value of x if the height relates to x by a proportion in similar triangles?

  A. 10/3
  B. 15/4 ✓ CORRECT
  C. 25/6
  D. 5

Explanation: Area = (1/2)(12)(y) = 6y. 48 ≤ 6y ≤ 60 means 8 ≤ y ≤ 10. For integer y = 8, 9, or 10, the corresponding x values are 10/3, 15/4, or 25/6 respectively.

Question 1021 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-vol-surf-46
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Area and volume
------------------------------------------------------------
A cube has an edge length of 6 inches. If the edge length is increased by 50%, by what percent does the surface area increase?

  A. 50%
  B. 100%
  C. 125% ✓ CORRECT
  D. 225%

Explanation: Original surface area = 6(6²) = 216. New edge = 9 inches. New surface area = 6(81) = 486. Increase = (486-216)/216 = 270/216 = 1.25 = 125%.


================================================================================

Question 1022 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-sector-47
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Circles
------------------------------------------------------------
A sector of a circle has a central angle of 120° and an arc length of 8π. What is the area of the sector?

  A. 24π
  B. 48π ✓ CORRECT
  C. 72π
  D. 96π

Explanation: Arc length = (θ/360)(2πr) = 8π. (120/360)(2πr) = 8π. (1/3)(2πr) = 8π. r = 12. Sector area = (θ/360)(πr²) = (1/3)(144π) = 48π.


================================================================================

Question 1023 [Math] - ID: sat-pdf-inscribed-48
Difficulty: Hard | Domain: Geometry and Trigonometry | Skill: Circles
------------------------------------------------------------
In a circle, an inscribed angle measures 35°. What is the measure of the central angle that subtends the same arc?

  A. 17.5°
  B. 35°
  C. 70° ✓ CORRECT
  D. 145°

Explanation: The central angle is always twice the inscribed angle that subtends the same arc. Central angle = 2(35°) = 70°.
